item.title: HOW TO SAVE MONEY — 43 replies — 152.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
HOW TO SAVE MONEY

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-23 00:49 UTC

OP

Euro here, I commute to work 2km on a bicycle. Never driven a car. I saved a fortune, missed some fun. Inflation is still gonna hit, but at least I dodged that massive bullet.

What do you do to save money?

Early replies (first 3)

62015679
>>62015674
Faggot

62015686
>>62015674
I am going by bus. Same. Time since the city is undrivable at the mornings and. Much cheaper.

62015687
>>62015686
Bus+metro

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62015759
>>62015757
Villa El Salvador

62015763
>>62015759
Ah yes, those were financed by Germany, I saw it on the page of our embassy in Lima when they were built. I hope our ambassador gets replaced soon, she's on a permanent holiday visiting all the nice places of Peru on our tax money.

62015765
>>62015763
So you are german? Nice, thank you very much and please don't replace her.

62015769
>>62015756
You are me.
I have bought and old house and renovated it totally on my own over the course of 5 years.
Saved probably 200k.
>>62015674
I cheat. I have an ebike. This is hill country here and i am very tired after work. 8km 250 m climb.

Additionally:
10 chickens. Eat table scraps lay 3-4 eggs a day feed is 20 bucks a month in winter.
We cook a lot with the instant pot. Throw the stuff in the instant pot at 10 pm or whenever you have the time, set timer. Finished meal at 1pm. or 2pm whatever it keeps it hot.
Also I have planted a ton of fruit trees and berries, of which I am now starting the reap the rewards. Jostaberry/Greengage/ quince are my best successes in zone 7b.

62015820
>>62015759
VES mentioned
holy based

62015903
>>62015674
>I commute to work 2km
Why even bike? The fuck?

62015995
Euro here, live in the capital of my country. Only ever driven my parents cars who live in a small town. In the capital i’ve never needed a car and i have never missed anything because of it. 72€ a month and i have unlimited transportation to anywhere in the metropolital region. My car owning friend says i’ve saved 10s of thousands of euros.

62016105
>>62015995
It's true. I spend 400€ on Diesel+Insurance for my car a month (~70km a day) Add 200€ for intitial Price + Maintenance. So 600€ to drive a shitbox to a place i hate and do stuff i loathe for net 2500€ a month. I'm a NEET now, because it's fucking stupid to live the wagie-life in this dupsterfire

62016150
>>62015995
And how much You spend on rent? Or do You live in Your own? I have my own house in rural hilly part of my country, commute 30km/day by car and insurance included I am like 100€/month. It still comes cheaper than renting in capital.

62016198
>>62016150
The ”city tax” at my city is like 1,5% lower than in my small hometown, i’m a high earner so that taken into account the rent really isn’t that high considering all the amenities i get from the big city.

62016236
Imagine being so poor that you “save money” by restricting your ability to travel to a few miles while being exposed to the elements. Sure seems peasant coded


I enjoy seeing bikers dealing with rain in freezing temperatures while I casually drive by in my heated leather seats listening to music on my surround sound system. Maybe today I will take a casual hundred mile drive just to go see some sites, can’t imagine having to deal with shitty slow public transit just to do something so basic, but bikers have no choice.

62016298
>>62015724
Holy shit, are you Bizzaro America?
Left....being good?
Right....retards?

62016368
>>62016236

>>62016236
You are fat. We get it.

62018180
How do I save money?

Eurofag here. Czech me out:
> live in 400k city, nice neighbor
> I have no car, use public transport
> only work from home, because I can, the office is over 30 min away and I hate people around me
> no subscriptions, no netflix
But
> wife has a car I pay for (bought it, pay for gas, service and insurance)
> I pay for her public transport pass
> she drives it to her job that is 1 bus stop (3 min) from our home and drops our kid to school that is 2 bus stops (7 min) the other way on the same line
> I pay for most of our groceries and all of our food delivery and restaurants
> I pay for most of our vacations (every year 2 weeks abroad, multiple 2-4day cabin rentals)

>>62015674

OP

Buy $1 worth of Ankh

The blockchain it's built on (ankh.cash) was re-released, after the original 2006 abstract for it and all its web archive records were scrubbed in November 2025. Then on February 28 2026, right before the Iran - Israel war, it was made open source on Github.

It's a biometric blockchain that began verifying identities a few days later per the nodes and blockchain data.

After that, a sovereign token and sidechain was deployed on it named Republic of Adal in Somalia (registered website adal.so) in the region of the bab al mandab strait.

There's a dormant ETH bridge confirmed to be related to it as per ankh.cash. An interest group is carving out this nation and managing its finances on the blockchain and vying for a grip of power on the shipping lanes which has an increased value due to strait of hormuz.

The value of the mainnet Ankh is designed to be a stablecoin pegged to USD/USDT. Inevitably the price will have to go up to $1 on the contract address. The currency for the Republic of Adal will be on this chain.

There are converging mass verification events in parts of the developing world using this same blockchain. A shift is taking place in the global order - and a biometric based blockchain built on a UBI protocol is the perfect recipe for total control of finances, albeit in the developing world.

My theory is they are done with the developed world and are looking for new pawns to control. Everything is intentional and positioning for power. I believe the end goal is intentionally devaluing, or hedging against the devaluation, of the US dollar. There's a small window where the value will be convertible and the surest bet is through the ETH bridged contract. After that...

the final weaves of the webs of the new world order are woven.

item.title: I've locked myself out of $900 and I need your help. — 32 replies — 17.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've locked myself out of $900 and I need your help.

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-03-22 23:14 UTC

OP

I've locked myself out of $900 and I need your help.

I use this exchange MEXC often as a simple middleman whenever I accept crypto from people in real life. So instead of giving you my address, I give people the address from the exchange and when I withdraw to my personal wallet it comes out of a different wallet so the people I interact with don't see all my tokens, activity, etc. Today I tried this shit and MEXC says I need to confirm my identity. If this was less than $100 I would just forget it but this is $900 so I thought ok fine I will do it. The problem is that they don't accept users from the USA so I was using a VPN and now they want to verify my ID from one of the countries that they accept users from. What do I do? Please I'm not going to be homeless from this but it really hurts to know that I just burned $900 dollars.

Early replies (first 3)

62014397
>>62014384
>I just burned $900

62014403
>>62014384

62014419
Come on is nobody really going to give me some kind of advice? I feel kind of depressed from this.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62014448
>>62014419
Give it back Jamal

62014453
This is not the right basket weaving forum for you if losing 900 Dollars depresses you.

62014462
>>62014384
Do you know somebody in a country from where Mexc accept users that you can trust?
If not, you are never goint to get that money back.
You should not fuck around with the laws when putting cash in one of these iffy exchanges.
Why didnt you use a Us based exchange? Or a metamask wallet?

62014478
>>62014384
be glad its only 900
try to be more careful next time

62014483
>>62014462
most likely because what he's doing is illegal

62014577
>>62014384
That's the MEXC gamble. It works fine on VPN until one day for no apparent reason it'll press you about confirming your identity and then you're fucked. I lost more than that on top of what I lost being a retard using leverage. I take it as a blessing considering had I not been banned, I probably would have added more margin and still have open positions currently as the market crabs. Fuck that site.

62014644
It's a shame we let these govs and companies ever get away with this ID verification shit to this point, isn't it?

62014783
>>62014644
Spain just required all citizens to report everty bit of property they have outside the country. (Land, stocks, cash, crypto,..)

62015121
>>62014462
>>62014483
I'm not doing anything illegal. I already told you the truth. I was using this exchange's wallets as a middle point so that people couldn't track my personal wallet. I bought $900 from a friend today and I gave him this CEX wallet without realizing that MEXC had changed their policies sometime in 2025. The reason I was using MEXC is because they don't require KYC and still let you withdraw like 1 whole bitcoin.

62015137
>>62014384
Stop being a fucking retard, I had the same shit happen to me. You write support email stating that all purchases were made on vacation in Mexico and if they don't let you withdraw you will get your lawyer involved. Its been a long time but mexc doesn't have any rules on people transferring crypto only buying and selling is prohibited for US citizens. Confirm your identity drop and write them that email and they will let you withdraw

62015140
>>62014384
How many times must we say it? Not your keys, not your coins.

62015141
>>62015137
What I meant is that buying crypto is illegal in the USA but not illegal for Americans on mexc so you can be American and buy crypto in Mexico on MEXC
Apologies I'm high as fuck but this worked for me

62015160
>>62015121
monero solves this

62015162
>>62015160
buy an ad.

62015175
>click on a perp dex to see what it's about
>get this message
I'm actually offended, I haven't tried to access shit. Little bitches.

62015216
>>62014384
Didn't you say your friend from another country was the person using it and you you were mistaken that it was you and you submitted his information to help him because he has a hard time with technology, and that he owes you 100% of the crypto in the wallet and is going to send it to your new master wallet as soon as it is unlocked for him by this exchange? I could've swore this was implied earlier?

62015253
>>62014384
Fellow burger here. I had this happen with $47k after rapidly depositing and swapping to xmr and trying to withdraw. I was being hugely retarded but I wasn't aware of better options for xmr at the time and I was fomoing into a parabolic move. They locked the account and it seemed like a hassle so I left the 47k usdt I hadnt yet withdrawn sitting there for 6 months or so. Finally I got around to going through kyc kabuki with them a few weeks ago. It was, as expected, drawn-out and humiliating but at the end they unlocked my acc and I withdrew with no further problems. So just email support, explain you're a burger who didn't know better and go through kyc via email with them. You'll have to live with the spectre of not knowing if theyve sold your kyc data to the darkweb but that's the price you have to pay, unfortunately.

62015297
>>62014462
>>62015141
>>62015216
>>62015253
Thanks to everybody for your advice. I will try to find someone from another country but if I can't then I guess I will email them and tell them I am from America. But won't they freeze my money since I've already made a few trades in the past? I have actually used their exchange in the past to make a few trades, but I very rarely trade on it.

62015310
>>62015297
I made trades into xmr and had several successful withdrawals to my xmr wallet before they froze my acct. They still unfroze me just fine after kabuki theatre had concluded. It probably helped that all my funds originated from a solana wallet I owbed with a ton of obvious shitcoin gains, so the source of my funds was relatively clear and straightforward. If you're getting random deposits from multiple addresses not under your control that might make your situation a lot murkier. Part of my humiliation ritual involved me filming a video of me showing that I controlled the wallet the funds originated from.

62015474
>>62015310
I don't own the wallet that sent the funds to MEXC. My friend owns the wallet and I gave him $900 so he would send the eth to me.

62015875
>>62015310
They let you use the account afterwards or just to withdraw?

62016008
>>62014384
It's bad if multiple wallets send coins to your exchange wallet because they can send funds from blacklisted wallets and the exchange could request proof of origin.
You should continue posting your issue here and on other communities. Proof your innocence. Be cooperative but if they drag it out, threaten endless FUD and complaints.

MEXC is kind of dirty and states KYC is optional but they freeze accounts randomly and then force KYC. This is called shotgun KYC.
Apparently they have recently made it mandatory without informing their users about this change.
The chances of your KYC data getting sold or leaked in the future are high. Already happened to T1 CEXs.

Before you use the identity of someone else, know that they could request more documents like bank statements or more videos.

>>62015253
There was a riot against MEXC on Twitter some months ago, maybe the have only let you withdraw because the pressure was too high?

62016019
>>62014384
Exchanges aren't stupid. They scan all your Android apps.
VPN apps and no KYC.
Bingo! We'll just scam this user selectively.

Anyway, your story is a load of bullshit. If you're in the US, you can easily get the same wallet address on Coinbase as on MEXC.
You're doing this to evade taxes.
At least be honest about it.

62016111
>>62015875
Only to withdraw.

62016598
>>62016008
No bank account was ever used so if I find someone from overseas they should have all the information required, even if MEXC demands video.
>>62016019
I never used my phone, only my desktop with a vpn. Why are you so certain I was doing illegal things? I am trying to evade taxes on $900? Do you hear yourself? As I've stated before, the truth is that I was using it because it is a buffer between me and whoever I give my address to, and the main reason I don't use Coinbase is because MEXC doesn't require KYC. Why would I give my ID to companies that will probably leak it eventually if I don't have to? It's literally just one extra step that I don't need to concern myself with. Also years ago I was trying to fomo in to something and I bought ETH on Coinbase and when I tried to withdraw it said I needed to wait a full 7 days. I don't know if this has changed since, but that was the last time I ever used Coinbase.

62016605
>>62014384
>he fell for the MEXC scam

62017262
get an rns id, i think the cheapest one is $300

use rns id to kyc at mexc

basically like getting $600 back which is better than nothing. or take your chances with mexc support

62017298
>>62014384
thanks for playing kiddo

62018015
>>62017262
Interesting, I've never heard about this before. I probably won't do it, but it sounds kind of cool. I will look into it.
